title: To prohibit the use of Federal financial assistance for a certain high-speed rail development project in the State of California, and for other purposes.
plain_summary: This bill stops the federal government from giving money to California for its high-speed rail project. The bill targets any project that is the same or very similar to a specific agreement made in 2011 between California's rail authority and the federal railroad agency. California has been trying to build a high-speed train system that can go over 200 miles per hour between Los Angeles and San Francisco. The federal government already ended the 2011 agreement in 2019, but this bill would prevent future federal funding for similar projects.
last_action_text: Referred to the Subcommittee on Railroads, Pipelines, and Hazardous Materials.

sections.title: Section 1  Prohibition on Provision of Federal Financial Assistance for Certain High-Speed Rail Development Project
sections.text: The federal government cannot give any money to the state of California for a high-speed train project that is the same as or very similar to a specific project. This specific project was created under an agreement called Cooperative Agreement Number FR-HSR-0118-12-01-01. This agreement was made between the California High-Speed Rail Authority (the state agency that runs California's high-speed rail project) and the Federal Railroad Administration (the federal agency that oversees railroads).
